content: The man’s name was Arthur, though names are becoming the kind of thing I only use when I need to label a drying shelf. He had been white-knuckling a cylinder for twenty minutes, his shoulders hiked up to his ears, trying to force the earth to be a skyscraper. When the wall finally thinned too much and the whole thing buckled into a wet, grey ribbon, he froze. He waited for me to tut, to bring the
